title: Platform engineering for the Agentic OS.
lead: BookMyShow: a million people, one show, one second. The infra doesn't get to fail. The build: an internal Agentic OS — a platform that lets AI agents plug into company workflows and take the repetitive operational work off the team.

beats.caption: LAYER 1 · INFRA
beats.body: DevOps at ticketing scale: pipelines, reliability, and infrastructure engineered to hold when a hundred thousand sessions spike at once.

beats.caption: LAYER 2 · PLATFORM
beats.body: The Agentic OS platform layer: a runtime that wires AI agents into internal tools and workflows so the agents can act on the company's own data and systems.

beats.caption: LAYER 3 · AUTOMATION
beats.body: Ops that used to need a person and a calendar now run on their own. Less manual work; more time for the work that actually needs a human.

beats.caption: BUILT · CODEBASE SCORING ENGINE
beats.title: 100-point codebase health, from the inside out.
beats.body: An in-house scoring algorithm that extracts resources from a codebase and returns a single score out of 100: a diagnostic for overall code quality and a clear starting point for what to fix next.

payoff.title: AGENTS RUN THE OPS.
payoff.sub: agentic platform engineering · devops · pipelines · ticketing scale

title: 20+ enterprise AI agents. In production.
lead: Enterprise AI agents on Azure (Agno framework), wired into real corporate systems over RESTful microservices. Each agent handles a slice of the work that used to go to a human.

beats.caption: THE STACK
beats.body: Azure · Agno framework · RESTful microservices · cloud-native from day one.

beats.caption: THE CLIENTS
beats.body: Real enterprise clients with live operational workflows — not prototypes. Every agent runs in a live corporate environment.

beats.caption: THE NUMBERS
beats.body: Manual workload down 60%. Throughput up 40%. The agents made the math better in both directions.

captionTail: KLIV GROUP, IIT KHARAGPUR · DL RESEARCH INTERN · JAN 2025 –
title: Big models. Tiny machines. Real data.
lead: 17 CNNs and Transformers. 8 input resolutions. 4 edge platforms. Not a one-shot experiment — a systematic benchmarking study built to actually answer the question: what does quantization cost, and where?

beats.caption: THE GRID
beats.body: Every model run against every resolution on every device. PTQ and QAT, INT8 throughout. The combinatorics were the point — no cherry-picking a single lucky config.

beats.caption: THE MEASUREMENTS
beats.body: Each run instrumented end-to-end: latency, throughput, activation memory, page fault rate. Plotted, cross-compared, reproduced. The kind of data that turns "it depends" into a real number.
beats.metric: latency · throughput · page faults · activation size

beats.caption: THE FINDING
beats.body: 35% faster inference. Accuracy above 90%. Model footprint down 40%. Reproducible across hardware — not one run on one device on a good day.

payoff.title: THE DATA SAYS IT FITS.
payoff.sub: jetson nano · coral edgetpu · edge benchmarking · INT8 PTQ + QAT · read the one-shot →

captionTail: TEAM ROBOMANIPAL, MIT MAHE · ROBOTICS SOFTWARE ENGINEER · MAR 2024 –
title: A robot needs to know exactly where it is, twenty times a second.
lead: Real-time 6-DOF pose estimation in ROS + OpenCV. The season, compressed into three competitions:

beats.caption: BEAT 1 · NATIONALS
beats.body: ABU Robocon 2024. Systematic testing, systematic validation, almost-perfect sheet.

beats.caption: BEAT 2 · GERMANY
beats.body: The same robot, an ocean away. RoboCup@Work 2025 international finals.

beats.caption: BEAT 3 · BRAZIL
beats.body: The same robot, another hemisphere. It still knows exactly where it is.

payoff.title: THE ROBOT KNOWS WHERE IT IS.
payoff.sub: 6-DOF pose estimation · 20+ FPS · millimeter accuracy · two continents
